creator: danny@dannywarren.com
text:
That is a fair point, Cy.  How would we go about collecting that information so that you aren't stuck in an endless loop of turning knobs?

My main issue is with changing the "hardstatus" behavior by default.  That is a pretty big change from screen's default behavior, and anyone expecting that functionality to work the same on freebsd out of the box is going to be surprised.

There are tradeoffs to changing the hardstatus type, and some deeper TERM functionality (such as sending the window name to xterm/putty/etc) breaks when you use "lastline" or "alwayslastline".

Breaking that out of the box just to visually re-skin screen seems like a bit of an overstep, and maybe best left to a user's personal screenrc or alternate sample config?
